page: Amazon Web Services i8ge.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, storage-optimized server powered by 96 dedicated physical cores of the ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor architecture running at 2.8 GHz. The system features 768 GB of DDR5 memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It includes a substantial local storage capacity of 60,000 GB delivered via NVMe SSDs, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core scores, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression all ranking in the top 10% of tested systems. The DDR5 memory subsystem achieves top-tier read and write bandwidth under large block sizes. Database and web operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by top-tier results in Passmark database operations, Redis SET operations reaching 199.43 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput. However, single-core CPU performance and small-model LLM text generation speeds are relatively lower, placing in the average and poor tiers respectively.

Given its bare-metal architecture, massive local NVMe storage, and high memory-to-core ratio, this server offers high resource density for data-intensive workloads. It is highly cost-efficient for large-scale databases, high-throughput transactional systems, and big data analytics where local storage speed and memory capacity are critical. The absence of hardware GPU accelerators makes it less suitable for heavy deep learning training, though it demonstrates strong text generation performance for medium and large LLMs. Appropriate workloads include distributed databases, NoSQL stores, large-scale caching layers, and high-volume web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, storage-optimized server designed for data-intensive workloads. Powered by 96 dedicated physical ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores operating at 2.8 GHz, it features 768 GB of DDR5 memory and an extensive 60,000 GB local NVMe SSD storage array. The server delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps. Benchmark results highlight top-tier multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and exceptional database execution speeds, with Redis SET operations reaching 199.43 million operations per second. While single-core performance is average, the platform's massive local storage capacity and high memory density provide excellent qualitative cost efficiency for scale-out databases, high-throughput transactional systems, and large-scale caching layers.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.metal-24xl is a bare-metal server with 96 ARMv9 Graviton cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 60 TB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for high-throughput database and storage-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.metal-24xl is a bare-metal server featuring 96 Graviton cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 60 TB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-capacity bare-metal server combining ninety-six ARMv9 cores with sixty terabytes of local NVMe storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 96 dedicated ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores at 2.8 GHz.
Equipped with 768 GB of DDR5 memory and 60,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Delivers 150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and Redis database performance.
Best suited for distributed databases, NoSQL stores, and large-scale caching layers.
